title: Cloud VM Unexpected Shutdown Due to Spot Instance Reclamation
symptoms: Batch processing VM terminated without application-initiated shutdown
EC2 instance state changed to terminated with spot interruption notice
Batch job status showing incomplete at 75% progress
No checkpoint data saved for job resumption
CloudWatch showing 2-minute spot interruption warning was unhandled
root_cause: Critical long-running batch workload incorrectly deployed on spot instances without checkpointing, instance diversification, or fallback to on-demand capacity
resolution: Restarted the batch job on an on-demand instance, migrated all critical batch workloads off spot instances, and implemented job checkpointing for future resilience
preventive_actions: Migrate all critical batch workloads to on-demand or reserved instances
Implement job checkpointing every 15 minutes for batch processing
Add spot interruption handling with automatic migration to on-demand
Create workload classification policy for spot vs on-demand usage
Implement batch job progress monitoring with alerts for stalled jobs

timeline.event: Batch job monitoring detects stalled progress
Lisa Park investigates, confirms spot reclamation
Decision to restart job on on-demand instance
On-demand instance provisioned, batch job restarted from scratch
Batch job completed on on-demand instance
Financial reconciliation data validated and delivered

embedding_summary: Critical financial reconciliation batch job interrupted by AWS spot instance reclamation on batch-processor-spot-12. 3 hours of processing lost due to missing checkpointing. Q1 financial close delayed by one business day. Resolved by restarting on on-demand instance and migrating critical workloads off spot.
